meta.title: Nicole — IGCSE/IB Entrance Readiness Diagnostic (Russian-L1 focus)
meta.author: Claude Code (CLI session)
meta.template_lineage: Same architecture as ernest_igcse_entrance_diagnostic_v1 (clusters + per-item L1-trap IDs + recognition/production split + offline scoring + 3/3/3 MCQ answer-position balance), recalibrated for Nicole: pitched lower (A2 core, A1 floor, B1 stretch — capped at B1, no B2), ~20 items + short writing, K-pop/school/friends themes, Russian-language run and debrief.
meta.purpose: Reveal Nicole's strengths and weaknesses ahead of the family's move to Kazakhstan and her entrance/placement test at a Cambridge IGCSE / IB school. Finished Year 8 at British School of Bahrain → entering ~Year 9 (KS3/MYP). Diagnostic, not drill.
meta.calibration_note: Item weighting is DATA-DRIVEN from players/nicole.coach_notes.weak_patterns (2026-07-01), not generic L1: heavier on question formation (do-support), irregular past participle / 3rd form, articles ('most of the', a+uncountable), much/many, than/that, and the 'feel myself' reflexive calque. Prepositions are a measured STRENGTH (86%) so only one light check is included. Irregular-verb PRODUCTION limited to a single item per her profile rule ('no irregular-verb input items until accuracy >70%'); the 3rd-form gap is probed mainly by recognition.
meta.level_design: A2 core, A1 floor probes, B1 stretch. Hard cap at B1 (level_cap per profile) — no B2 items.
instructions_for_test_taker_ru: Один заход, примерно 25 минут. Сама — без словаря и без помощи.
Один ответ на вопрос. Не возвращайся назад менять ответы.
Для русских предложений напиши естественный английский, не слово-в-слово.
Если совсем не знаешь — напиши PASS и иди дальше. PASS — это нормально.
instructions_for_scorer: Score each item correct / partial / incorrect. Tag every miss with its l1_trap_id.
Compute per-cluster correctness AND the recognition-vs-production split (her documented articles + question-formation gaps are recognition-vs-production shaped).
For irregular verbs, separate past-simple (expected OK) from 3rd-form/past-participle (expected weak) when reading results — don't collapse them.
Debrief + any student-facing materials in RUSSIAN, simple vocab, warm tone, occasional emoji (feedbackDepth: medium-kid). Score the writing for L1-trap density, not style.
meta.answer_position_balance: Correct MCQ options spread 3/3/3 across a/b/c. a = n_irr_01, n_art_01, n_cond_01; b = n_irr_02, n_cmp_02, n_tense_01; c = n_prep_01, n_q_03, n_ca_01.

clusters.question_formation: Do-support + auxiliary order in questions. Documented 0% weak spot (qf03/06/07).
clusters.irregular_verbs: Past participle / 3rd form unstable (supplies past-simple form: drove→driven). Past simple itself is OK — one floor item confirms that.
clusters.articles: No article system in Russian. Superlative 'the', 'most of THE students', 'a' on uncountables.
clusters.countability_agreement: much/many (documented weak), third-person do-support agreement, irregular plurals (children).
clusters.comparisons: than vs that (documented cp01 confusion), comparative form (better not more good).
clusters.tense_aspect: Present simple vs present continuous for actions happening now (Russian has one present tense).
clusters.conditionals: Zero/first conditional; no 'will' in the if-clause (Russian marks future in both halves).
clusters.prepositions: Time on/in/at. MEASURED STRENGTH — one light check only.

format_legend.gap_mcq: English stem with ___; choose one option.
format_legend.error_correction: Stem has one L1-typical error; rewrite it correctly.
format_legend.ru_to_en: Russian sentence; write the natural English.

items.stem: BTS is ___ most famous group in Korea.
items.explanation: Superlatives take 'the' — 'the most famous group'. Russian has no articles, so 'the' gets dropped after the superlative word.

items.stem: Yesterday I ___ to school by bus.
items.explanation: Past of 'go' is 'went' (irregular). Floor item — past simple is expected to be solid for her; confirms the gap is in the 3rd form, not the past.

items.stem: My dance class is ___ Monday.
items.explanation: Days take 'on' — 'on Monday'. Compare 'in the morning', 'at 3 o'clock'. Prepositions are her strength — this is a light confirmation only.

items.stem: Which sentence is correct?
items.explanation: Yes/no questions invert with 'do': 'Do you like dancing?'. 'You like dancing?' (statement + question mark) is the Russian-style pattern. Targets her documented do-support gap.

items.stem: How ___ friends do you have?
items.explanation: 'Friends' are countable → 'how many friends'. 'Much' is for uncountables (how much money). Russian doesn't split much/many. Documented weak spot (mm03/mm05).

items.stem: Be quiet! I ___ to my favourite song right now.
items.explanation: Happening right now → present continuous 'am listening', not present simple 'listen'. Russian uses one present tense for both.

items.stem: If it ___ tomorrow, we will stay home.
items.explanation: First conditional: 'if' + present simple, main clause 'will' — 'If it rains..., we will stay'. Don't put 'will' in the if-part. Russian marks the future in both halves.

items.stem: I think this song is ___ than the old one.
items.explanation: 'Good' is irregular → 'better', not 'more good' or 'gooder'. Comparative form is a documented weak area.

items.stem: I have ___ that song so many times that I know all the words.
items.explanation: Present perfect needs the 3rd form: 'have sung', not 'have sang' (past simple). This is her exact documented pattern — the past-simple form is stable, the 3rd form is not. 'sang' is the trap.

items.stem: Where you live now?
items.explanation: English questions need do-support: 'Where do you live?'. Russian uses intonation with no auxiliary. Her documented 0% weak spot.

items.stem: Most of students in my class like K-pop.
items.answer: Most of the students in my class like K-pop.
items.explanation: 'Most of' + a specific group needs 'the': 'most of the students'. Russian drops the article. Her exact documented pattern (mp02).

items.stem: My brother don't like K-pop.
items.explanation: Third-person singular needs 'doesn't': 'My brother doesn't like...'. Russian has no do-support, so 'don't' leaks in for he/she.

items.stem: My phone is better that yours.
items.answer: My phone is better than yours.
items.explanation: Comparisons use 'than', not 'that': 'better than yours'. Russian 'чем' has no English homophone, so 'than/that' get confused. Her documented cp01 pattern.

items.stem: Look! It rains outside.
items.explanation: Right now → present continuous 'it's raining', not 'it rains'. Russian present tense covers both, so the simple form is the L1 default.

items.stem: I feel myself happy when I dance.
items.answer: I feel happy when I dance.
items.explanation: 'Feel' + adjective takes no reflexive: 'I feel happy'. Russian 'чувствую себя' calques to 'feel myself'. Her documented fm01 pattern.

items.stem: I have never drove a car.
items.answer: I have never driven a car.
items.explanation: Present perfect needs the 3rd form: 'have driven', not 'have drove'. 'Drove' is the past-simple form she lexicalises — exactly the documented irvpp pattern. (Only irregular PRODUCTION item, per her profile rule.)

items.stem: What music you like?
items.explanation: 'What music do you like?' — questions need the auxiliary 'do'. Same do-support gap; a second surface confirms whether it's stable.

items.stem: The teacher gave us a homework for the weekend.
items.answer: The teacher gave us homework for the weekend.
items.explanation: 'Homework' is uncountable — no 'a', no plural: 'gave us homework'. Russian 'домашнее задание' can feel countable, so 'a homework' slips in.

items.stem: There are three childrens in the garden.
items.answer: There are three children in the garden.
items.explanation: Plural of 'child' is 'children' (irregular) — no extra -s. Same family: man→men, mouse→mice, tooth→teeth.

items.stem_ru: Если ты придёшь, мы посмотрим фильм.
items.answer: If you come, we will watch a film.
accept: If you come, we'll watch a movie.
items.explanation: First conditional: present in the if-part ('If you come'), 'will' in the main part. Russian marks the future in both halves, giving 'if you will come'.

items.stem: Write 4-6 sentences about your favourite music or K-pop group, and what you like doing at the weekend.
items.scorer_note: Free-production surface. Score for L1-trap density, not style. Watch for: articles (the/a/zero on group names, 'the weekend'), present simple habits vs present continuous, question-formation isn't triggered here but do-support/agreement in statements is ('my friends like', 'she sings'), much/many, and any 'feel myself' calque. Debrief in Russian.
